name: Austin Grill's Chili Verde
recipeIngredient: 5 whole fresh Anaheim chile, seeded, peeled
1 Idaho potato -- medium -- peeled, cut into 1/2 inch dice.
2 cups chicken breast, uncooked, cut into 1/2 inch dice
1 cup cilantro, loosely packed, large stems removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut Anaheim and poblano chiles in half, remove the seeds, char, and peel.
2. In a large skillet, heat olive oil and sauté onion until translucent.
3. Add garlic, jalapeno chiles, cumin, and oregano; sauté for several minutes.
4. Add chicken stock and bring to a simmer. Add potatoes and cook until just tender.
5. Using a slotted spoon, remove 1 cup of potatoes and reserve. Add diced chicken and poach gently in the onion-chili-potato mixture until cooked through.
6. Remove from heat immediately, transfer to a bowl, and reserve half cup of liquid in a small bowl. Refrigerate the remainder to prevent the chicken from overcooking.
7. In a blender, puree the reserved potatoes, half of the Anaheim and poblano chilies, and cilantro with the reserved liquid.
8. When the chicken mixture is no longer warm, add the puree. Dice the remaining half of the Anaheim and poblano chiles into 1/2 inch cubes and add to the mixture.
9. Season with salt. Reheat just before serving to retain the fresh green color and flavor of the soup.
